quality: Saving sacred knowledge from flood / navigating chaos
story_role: Rescuer from the deep; preserver of wisdom
character_archetype: The guide who carries what must not be lost
notes: Appears in stories of hidden knowledge and underwater secrets

quality: Patient foundation; bearing the weight of worlds; endurance without complaint
story_role: The silent support — holds the cosmos while others act
character_archetype: The one who bears the unbearable quietly
notes: For heroes who are the foundation that nobody notices

quality: Rescuing the earth from darkness; diving into the abyss to retrieve what was lost
story_role: The one who plunges into the dark to save what is sacred
character_archetype: The protector who descends rather than soars
notes: For stories where the hero must enter the villain's world to win

quality: Unstoppable righteous fury; protection of the devoted; destroying what seems indestructible
story_role: The rage that breaks all limits when dharma is threatened
character_archetype: The protector who becomes terrifying to protect
notes: Gujarat hero (Simhavikrama) is most directly connected

form: Small brahmin boy who becomes cosmic giant
quality: Humility hiding infinite power; claiming what is rightfully dharmic through cleverness not force
story_role: The one who appears weak but encompasses everything
character_archetype: The hero who is underestimated and then reveals cosmic scale
notes: For heroes who are overlooked but carry world-changing power

quality: Warrior-sage; righteous anger against corrupt power; the axe that clears before the city can be built
story_role: The destroyer of corrupt old order so new dharma can grow
character_archetype: The warrior who never smiles — duty above all else
notes: Kerala hero (Parashurama) directly named after this avatar

quality: Dharma as personal cost; leadership through sacrifice; faithfulness to vow above personal happiness
story_role: The king who suffers for his people's sake
character_archetype: The hero whose greatest strength is also their tragedy
notes: For heroes who must choose duty over desire

quality: Divine love + cosmic wisdom; the one who guides without fighting; truth told in paradox
story_role: The guide who reveals the infinite in the ordinary
character_archetype: The hero who is always one step beyond — never fully grasped
notes: Most referenced avatar in MahaHulmane — Manipur and UP heroes connect here

quality: Compassion over conquest; seeing through illusion; non-violence as the highest power
weapon_or_symbol: None — the teaching is the weapon
story_role: The one who transforms enemies by understanding them
character_archetype: The hero who wins without fighting — the stillness that is invincible
notes: Sikkim hero (Kanchenjunga) most closely reflects this

form: White horse warrior with flaming sword
quality: The coming reckoning; time's arrow; the justice that arrives when all else has failed
story_role: The future warrior who ends an age and begins the next
character_archetype: The hero who represents what must eventually come — inevitable dharmic justice
notes: The Void Maw arc is the Kalki-equivalent moment for MahaHulmane as a whole
